content:
Any idea why I see this error?

  DESCEND objtool
  UPD     include/config/kernel.release
  UPD     include/generated/utsrelease.h
  CALL    scripts/checksyscalls.sh
  RUSTC H rust/libkparser.rlib
grep: warning: stray \ before #
  RUSTC L rust/bindings.o
error[E0588]: packed type cannot transitively contain a `#[repr(align)]` type
    --> /home/vincent/gittea/linux/rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs:4746:1
     |
4746 | / pub struct alt_instr {
4747 | |     pub instr_offset: s32,
4748 | |     pub repl_offset: s32,
4749 | |     pub __bindgen_anon_1: alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1,
4750 | |     pub instrlen: u8_,
4751 | |     pub replacementlen: u8_,
4752 | | }
     | |_^
     |
note: `alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1__bindgen_ty_1` has a `#[repr(align)]` attribute
    --> /home/vincent/gittea/linux/rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs:4763:1
     |
4763 | / pub struct alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1__bindgen_ty_1 {
4764 | |     pub _bitfield_1: __BindgenBitfieldUnit<[u8; 4usize], u16>,
4765 | | }
     | |_^
note: `alt_instr` contains a field of type `alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1`
    --> /home/vincent/gittea/linux/rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs:4749:9
     |
4749 |     pub __bindgen_anon_1: alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1,
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
note: ...which contains a field of type `alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1__bindgen_ty_1`
    --> /home/vincent/gittea/linux/rust/bindings/bindings_generated.rs:4756:9
     |
4756 |     pub __bindgen_anon_1: alt_instr__bindgen_ty_1__bindgen_ty_1,
     |         ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

error: aborting due to previous error

For more information about this error, try `rustc --explain E0588`.
make[1]: *** [rust/Makefile:412: rust/bindings.o] Error 1
make[1]: *** Waiting for unfinished jobs....
make: *** [Makefile:1293: prepare] Error 2

The bottom ones are an issue that is sometimes hit with bindgen (repr(packed) inside repr(align) type), you will need an opaque type or to add it to the denylist, but if I am guessing correctly and this is for kprocmacros, I think you should not be seeing that because you are not changing something related, right? Are you based on top of rust or something else?
As for the top one, grep: warning: stray \ before #, I guess you are missing a newline somewhere (or tried to add a comment somewhere it does not work, e.g. one of the variable assignments).
I guess the top one could be the root cause of the bottom one, depending on what the issue with the Makefile is, if you are trying to integrate kprocmacros.

Miguel Ojeda said:

The bottom ones are an issue that is sometimes hit with bindgen (repr(packed) inside repr(align) type), you will need an opaque type or to add it to the denylist, but if I am guessing correctly and this is for kprocmacros, I think you should not be seeing that because you are not changing something related, right? Are you based on top of rust or something else?

I'm based on the current linus master https://git.hedwing.dev/vincenzopalazzo/linux/commit/1d2aea1bcf68992c90218f47405bee29efd722cd
I will try to compile a clean master with rust enable, let see what happens
looks like that this happens also t ome on a clean master, strange

I think my arch linux build machine find a grep bug

This fix the grep warning but not the compiler error 

diff --git a/rust/Makefile b/rust/Makefile
index f88d108fbef0..41ac8401a8be 100644
--- a/rust/Makefile
+++ b/rust/Makefile
@@ -283,7 +283,7 @@ quiet_cmd_bindgen = BINDGEN $@
                $(bindgen_target_cflags) $(bindgen_target_extra)

 $(obj)/bindings/bindings_generated.rs: private bindgen_target_flags = \
-    $(shell grep -v '^\#\|^$$' $(srctree)/$(src)/bindgen_parameters)
+    $(shell grep -v '^#\|^$$' $(srctree)/$(src)/bindgen_parameters)
 $(obj)/bindings/bindings_generated.rs: $(src)/bindings/bindings_helper.h \
     $(src)/bindgen_parameters FORCE
        $(call if_changed_dep,bindgen)
I see that you reported something similar https://github.com/rust-lang/rust-bindgen/issues/2179

Miguel Ojeda said:

As for the top one, grep: warning: stray \ before #, I guess you are missing a newline somewhere (or tried to add a comment somewhere it does not work, e.g. one of the variable assignments).

https://news.slashdot.org/story/22/09/05/0135254/

I remember I saw a few changes in other projects because of this recently

The bottom ones are an issue that is sometimes hit with bindgen (repr(packed) inside repr(align) type), you will need an opaque type or to add it to the denylist, but if I am guessing correctly and this is for kprocmacros, I think you should not be seeing that because you are not changing something related, right? Are you based on top of rust or something else?

Arnaldo made it opaque here https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/ZACvxNOuuyifQ9Nx@kernel.org/
